_design_notes: Every weapon tells a story. Common weapons are found, scavenged, or traded. Named weapons are gifted by NPCs, earned through quests, or discovered in sacred/historical sites. Legendary weapons are one-per-playthrough and tied to major narrative choices. No 'shop' sells high-tier weapons — Usagi is a persecuted pilgrim, not a fantasy adventurer.

katana.label: Katana
katana.description: Speed and precision. The blade path.

staff.label: Staff
staff.description: Balance and spirit. The pilgrim's path.

cross_blade.label: Cross-Blade
cross_blade.description: Faith as weapon. The holy warrior's path.

improvised.label: Improvised
improvised.description: Creativity and resilience. The farmer's path.

fist.label: Fist/Gauntlet
fist.description: Body as prayer. The ascetic's path.

spear.label: Spear/Polearm
spear.description: Reach and discipline. The soldier's path.

bow.label: Bow/Ranged
bow.description: Distance and patience. The watchman's path.

katana.name: Rusty Wakizashi
katana.found: Looted from bandit, found in abandoned house
katana.description: A short sword rusted nearly shut in its scabbard. It was someone's once. Now it's yours.
katana.lore: The stamp on the tang is unreadable. Whoever forged this didn't expect it to end up in a rabbit's paw.

katana.name: Village Tantō
katana.found: Gifted by Ichiro, traded for wolf pelt
katana.description: A short knife for gutting fish and cutting rope. It'll cut other things too, if you need it to.

katana.name: Pilgrim's Blade
katana.found: Given by the Messenger on the mountain summit
katana.description: Given by the Messenger. Its story is not yet written. It hums faintly during prayer.
katana.lore: Forged from your grandmother's prayers, the Messenger said. The steel is warm. It has not cooled since the mountain.

evolution_descriptions.battles_100: The handle has worn smooth under your grip. The balance has shifted — it feels like part of your arm now.
evolution_descriptions.battles_250: There are scratches on the blade you didn't make. They form patterns. If you tilt it in firelight, they look like words.
evolution_descriptions.battles_500: It sings. Not a sound anyone else can hear. A note that resonates in your bones when you pray.
evolution_descriptions.faith_10: The blade glows. Not hot, not cold. Alive. The Messenger's gift has become something more — a covenant in steel.

katana.name: Soldier's Katana
katana.found: Looted from defeated ashigaru, dropped by Captain Hideki's men
katana.description: Standard issue. Mass-produced for the daimyō's army. Reliable, soulless, sharp.
katana.moral_weight: Carrying the persecutor's weapon. The Accuser notices.

katana.name: Mori's Naginata
katana.found: Dropped by Commander Mori after defeating him
katana.description: Commander Mori's polearm. Carrying it feels wrong — or right, depending on who you've become.
katana.moral_weight: The Accuser will comment on this.

katana.name: Rōnin's Blade
katana.found: Traded from a wandering masterless samurai
katana.description: The sword of a masterless warrior. It has killed many. It does not judge who holds it next.

katana.name: Shimabara Rebel's Blade
katana.found: Given by rebel leader at Hara Castle
katana.description: Carried by a Christian farmer who became a soldier because he had no other choice. The cross is scratched into the pommel.
katana.lore: The Shimabara rebels were peasants and farmers. They knew they would lose. They fought anyway.

katana.name: Roman Gladius
katana.found: Taken from fallen legionary, given by Marcellus
katana.description: Short, broad, brutally efficient. Rome conquered the world with this blade. Twenty inches of destiny.

katana.name: Marcellus's Spatha
katana.found: Given by Marcellus if player convinces him to choose faith over violence
katana.description: The cavalry sword of a converted legionary. Marcellus carved a fish into the pommel the night he was baptized.
katana.lore: He said: 'I killed for Rome for twenty years. Now I'll die for Christ in twenty seconds. Fair trade.'

katana.name: Crusader's Longsword
katana.found: Standard Crusader armament, found in Acre armory
katana.description: Cross-hilted longsword. The crossguard is functional — and symbolic. Every swing is a benediction or a curse.

katana.name: Godfrey's Blade
katana.found: Given by Godfrey if Usagi earns his respect through defending the walls
katana.description: The knight-commander's personal sword. The blade is etched with the Psalm: 'Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death.'
katana.lore: Godfrey told Usagi: 'I don't give this lightly. This blade has never struck a man who didn't deserve it. Keep that record clean.'

katana.name: Polish Szabla
katana.found: Given by Polish cavalry before the Battle of Liegnitz
katana.description: Curved cavalry saber. Made for a mounted charge — and for the desperate last stand that always follows.

katana.name: Templar's Falchion
katana.found: Discovered in a Templar chapel vault
katana.description: Broad-bladed weapon of the Poor Knights of Christ. The vow of poverty is scratched into the blade. It's still sharp.
katana.lore: The Templars were warrior-monks. They prayed eight times a day and killed without hesitation. History hasn't decided whether they were saints or madmen.

katana.name: Lepanto Prize Scimitar
katana.found: Captured during the Battle of Lepanto
katana.description: Ottoman steel captured at Lepanto. Curved, fast, elegant. It doesn't care who holds it — only that they swing well.

katana.name: Resistance Fighter's Knife
katana.found: Given by the underground resistance in occupied Poland
katana.description: Short, practical, hidden in a boot. It has saved more lives than it has taken. That's rare for a knife.

katana.name: Echo of Kusanagi
katana.found: Hidden in Urakami Cathedral, accessible only with Faith ≥ 8
katana.description: Not the legendary sword itself — an echo. A memory of divine steel, preserved by centuries of prayer. It cuts through lies.
katana.lore: The original Kusanagi was a treasure of the emperor. This is its spiritual shadow, hidden by the kakure kirishitan beneath the altar of Urakami.

katana.name: Pilgrim's Blade Perfected
katana.found: Pilgrim's Blade evolves into this at level 80+ with Faith 10 and Devotion Grace tier 3
katana.description: It is no longer a blade. It is a prayer made visible. Every soul who held it lives in the steel. Obaa-chan's warmth. Peter's blessing. Kolbe's sacrifice. Your faithfulness.
katana.lore: The Messenger said it was forged from your grandmother's prayers. After two thousand years of carrying it, you understand: it was forged from everyone's prayers. Including yours.

staff.name: Walking Stick
staff.found: Picked up anywhere — it's a stick
staff.description: A stick. Cut from a tree. It helps you walk. In desperate times, it helps you fight.

staff.name: Bamboo Staff
staff.description: A length of hardened bamboo. Walking stick, weapon, and prayer focus.

staff.name: Oaken Pilgrim's Staff
staff.found: Common pilgrim equipment in European eras
staff.description: Sturdy oak, worn smooth by many hands. It has walked from Canterbury to Jerusalem.

staff.name: Franciscan Staff
staff.description: A walking staff, nothing more. Francis refused all weapons. 'The Lord is my strength.' This staff deals holy damage naturally based on SPR, not ATK.

staff.name: Iron-Shod Staff
staff.found: Crafted by a village blacksmith or found in monastery
staff.description: Oak staff with iron caps at both ends. The monks of the Middle Ages called it a 'persuader.'

staff.name: Desert Father's Staff
staff.found: Given by Anthony the Great after surviving his trial
staff.description: Acacia wood, bleached white by the Egyptian sun. Anthony held it for sixty years of solitary prayer. It hums with silence.
staff.lore: Anthony said: 'I don't fight demons. I stand still and let God fight through me. The staff helps me stand.'

staff.name: Benedictine Cross-Staff
staff.description: A staff topped with the Medal of St. Benedict. 'Vade retro Satana' — 'Begone, Satan.' The words are forged in bronze.

staff.name: Catacomb Torch-Staff
staff.description: Staff with an oil-soaked cloth head. Lights the tunnels of the dead. The living need light more.

staff.name: Hardwood Bō
staff.found: Crafted by village elder or martial arts monastery
staff.description: Six feet of hardened red oak. In skilled hands, it blocks swords. In any hands, it hurts.

staff.name: Templar Processional Cross
staff.found: Found in Templar chapel during the defense of Acre
staff.description: A tall processional cross used in the Templar liturgy. Heavy bronze and oak. It was carried into battle by the chaplain. The chaplain didn't come back.

staff.name: Shepherd's Crook
staff.found: Given by Father Linus, found in Egyptian village
staff.description: The bishop's symbol — the shepherd's staff. Not for striking wolves. For pulling sheep out of ravines.

staff.name: Broken Pike
staff.found: Scavenged from the streets of revolutionary Paris
staff.description: The shaft is snapped. The head is loose. Someone carried this to the Bastille and back. It's seen more history than most people.

staff.name: Kolbe's Crutch
staff.found: Found in Kolbe's cell after his death. He doesn't need it anymore.
staff.description: A crude wooden crutch. Maximilian Kolbe used it in the starvation bunker. He sang hymns while leaning on it. It is the weakest weapon in the game and the most powerful.
staff.lore: It deals almost no physical damage. Its SPR scaling is the highest in the game. Against spiritual enemies, it is devastating. Against physical enemies, you will struggle. This is the Franciscan paradox.

staff.name: Rod of Moses
staff.found: Appears in Usagi's hand during the final battle if he has Staff mastery 10 and Faith 10
staff.description: It parted the sea. It struck the rock and water flowed. It became a serpent and swallowed Pharaoh's magic. In Usagi's hands, it is all of these things.
staff.lore: The game does not explain where it came from. It is simply there when needed. That is how grace works.

cross_blade.name: Cross Dagger
cross_blade.found: Hidden behind the altar stone in the hidden chapel
cross_blade.description: A dagger with a cross-shaped guard. Hidden in the chapel altar stone. Deals holy damage.

cross_blade.name: Catacomb Cross-Blade
cross_blade.found: Found in the Roman catacombs, hidden in a niche with other sacred objects
cross_blade.description: Iron blade with cross-shaped pommel, wrapped in linen. Someone hid this here before their arrest. They never came back for it.

cross_blade.name: Jesuit Cross-Blade
cross_blade.description: A processional cross refitted as a weapon. The vertical beam is sharpened. It is both cross and blade — the Jesuit paradox.

cross_blade.name: Dominican Truth-Blade
cross_blade.description: 'The Sword of the Word.' A blade inscribed with Veritas on one side and John 8:32 on the other: 'The truth shall set you free.' Bonus damage against spiritual enemies using deception.

cross_blade.name: Martyr's Cross-Blade
cross_blade.found: Given by a martyr before their execution
cross_blade.description: The blade of one who chose death over denial. Below 30% HP, holy damage doubles. The cross glows brighter as hope dims.
cross_blade.lore: The martyr said: 'Take it. I won't need a weapon where I'm going. Only forgiveness.'

cross_blade.name: Nicene Blade
cross_blade.found: Blessed by Athanasius after defending the Nicene Creed
cross_blade.description: A blade forged in defense of the Trinity. The cross guard bears three interlocking rings — Father, Son, Spirit. Heretical enemies take +30% damage.

cross_blade.name: Crusader's Cross Sword
cross_blade.found: Standard Crusader armament with cross-shaped hilt
cross_blade.description: Standard Crusader issue. The cross-shaped hilt was designed to be kissed before battle. Most soldiers forgot.

cross_blade.name: Father Bernard's Blade
cross_blade.found: Given by Father Bernard if Usagi protects Salima's orphanage
cross_blade.description: The Franciscan chaplain's cross-blade. Bernard never used it to kill — only to defend. The edge is dull. The blessing is not.
cross_blade.lore: Bernard said: 'A dull blade that protects the innocent is sharper than any sword that draws blood.'

cross_blade.name: Inquisitor's Brand
cross_blade.moral_weight: Carries the weight of the Inquisition's history. NPCs react with fear.
cross_blade.description: Weapon of the Holy Office. It burned heretics and saved souls — or burned souls and saved nothing. History disagrees.

cross_blade.name: Lepanto Victory Cross
cross_blade.found: Forged from captured Ottoman cannon metal, blessed by fleet chaplain
cross_blade.description: Cast from the bronze of captured Ottoman cannons after the battle. The cross on the hilt is made from a melted-down crescent. War makes strange alchemy.

cross_blade.name: Carmelite Sisters' Cross
cross_blade.found: Found on the scaffold where the Carmelite nuns were executed
cross_blade.description: Sixteen nuns sang the Veni Creator as they climbed to the blade. This small cross was found where they stood. It still hums with their hymn.
cross_blade.lore: Equipping it plays a faint choral ambience. All allies gain +2 SPR while it is held.

cross_blade.name: Underground Church Cross
cross_blade.found: Hidden beneath floorboards in an occupied Polish parish
cross_blade.description: Crudely forged from scrap metal in a basement workshop. The Christians of occupied Poland made these in secret. It's ugly. It's sacred.

cross_blade.name: Urakami Cross
cross_blade.found: Found in the ruins of Urakami Cathedral in the final book
cross_blade.description: A cross-blade forged from the melted bells of Urakami Cathedral. The largest Christian community in Japan was destroyed by an atomic bomb dropped by a Christian nation. This cross remembers.
cross_blade.lore: Its existence is a paradox. Its power comes from that paradox — the refusal to let contradiction destroy faith.

cross_blade.name: Splinter of the True Cross
cross_blade.found: Appears only if Faith is 10, all virtues ≥ 5, and the Accuser has been faced in every book
cross_blade.description: A sliver of wood no larger than a thumbnail. It does not look like a weapon. It is the most dangerous object in the game.
cross_blade.lore: The True Cross was found by Helena in Jerusalem. Fragments were distributed across Christendom. This one found its way to a rabbit farmer across two thousand years. God has a sense of humor.

improvised.name: Farmer's Sickle
improvised.found: Starting weapon / found in any agricultural area
improvised.description: A rice-harvesting sickle. Not a weapon. But it has an edge.

improvised.name: Sharpened Hoe
improvised.description: A kuwa hoe with the edge filed sharp. It turns soil. It also turns skulls, if required.

improvised.name: Fishing Gaff
improvised.description: Iron hook on a wooden handle. Meant for hauling tuna. Improvise.

improvised.name: Grain Flail
improvised.description: A grain threshing flail. Two sticks connected by chain. Peasant armies across history have picked these up. They work.

improvised.name: Broken Oar
improvised.description: Half an oar. Heavy, splintered, desperate. The weapon of someone with no other options.

improvised.name: Kitchen Cleaver
improvised.description: For chopping vegetables. Allegedly. The blade is thick, heavy, and remarkably persuasive.

improvised.name: Weighted Rope
improvised.found: Crafted from rope and a stone
improvised.description: A river stone tied to a leather cord. Simple physics. The stone swings, the cord wraps, the enemy stumbles. 20% chance to bind for 1 turn.

improvised.name: Miner's Pickaxe
improvised.description: Iron pick on oak handle. Designed to break rock. Also effective against armor. 10% armor penetration.

improvised.name: Pitchfork
improvised.description: Three iron tines on a wooden shaft. The weapon of every peasant rebellion in history. There's a reason.

improvised.name: Burning Torch
improvised.description: A stick on fire. Illuminates, intimidates, and sets things ablaze. 5% chance to inflict Burn.

improvised.name: Blacksmith's Hammer
improvised.description: Heavy iron head, oak handle. Designed to shape metal. Shapes other things too. 10% stun chance.

improvised.name: Shepherd's Sling
improvised.found: Given by a shepherd boy in Egypt or the Holy Land
improvised.description: Leather and cord. Two river stones. David killed a giant with one of these. You probably won't. But the bonus against large enemies is real.
improvised.lore: Against enemies tagged 'large' or 'boss,' damage +25%. Faith in the impossible.

improvised.name: Rebel's Kusarigama
improvised.description: Sickle on a chain with a weighted ball. The weapon of the desperate and the creative. 15% disarm chance.

improvised.name: Guillotine Fragment
improvised.moral_weight: Carrying a piece of the guillotine. SPR penalty. The Accuser approves. That should worry you.
improvised.description: A shard of the blade that killed thousands. It cuts well. It always did. But holding it feels wrong in ways you can't explain.

improvised.name: Camp Shovel
improvised.description: Steel shovel from the camp. It dug graves. Now it digs something else — a way out, maybe. Or a place to bury the evidence.

improvised.name: Urakami Rebar
improvised.found: Pulled from the ruins of Urakami Cathedral
improvised.description: Steel rebar from the shattered cathedral. It held the building together. The building is gone. The steel remains. So does the faith.

improvised.name: Widow's Mite Sling
improvised.found: Appears when Usagi has fewer than 5 items total and Charity ≥ 8
improvised.description: A sling that grows stronger the less you carry. 'She gave everything she had.' When inventory is empty, ATK triples.
improvised.lore: The widow gave two small coins — all she had. Jesus said she gave more than the rich men. This weapon understands that math.

fist.name: Bare Hands
fist.description: God gave you two hands. Use them.

fist.name: Cloth-Wrapped Fists
fist.description: Strips of cotton wrapped tight around the knuckles. Protects your hands. Doesn't protect theirs.

fist.name: Leather Knuckle Guards
fist.description: Hardened leather over the knuckles and wrist. Keeps you punching when skin would split.

fist.name: Iron Knuckles
fist.description: Cast iron over the fist. Each punch lands with an extra pound of persuasion.

fist.name: Roman Cestus
fist.description: Leather wraps studded with metal spikes. Gladiators used these in the arena. The crowd loved the blood. 10% bleed chance.

fist.name: Battle Rosary
fist.found: Blessed prayer beads wrapped around the fist as a weapon
fist.description: Wooden rosary beads wrapped around the knuckles. Each punch is a Hail Mary. The enemy receives the full mystery.

fist.name: Monk's Prayer Wraps
fist.description: Linen strips inscribed with prayers. The monks wrap their hands for manual labor. The prayers soak into the skin.

fist.name: Crusader's Gauntlet
fist.description: Steel-plated fighting gauntlet. Designed for close quarters on castle walls. The cross is embossed on each knuckle.

fist.name: Stigmata Wraps
fist.found: Appears after witnessing the Francis vision (Charity ≥ 5)
fist.description: Bloodstained linen. The wounds of Christ appear on those who love Him most. These wraps glow when Usagi is struck — 10% of damage taken reflected as holy damage.

fist.name: Polish Rękawica
fist.found: Polish armory before Battle of Liegnitz
fist.description: Heavy steel fighting glove used by Polish knights. When the sword is gone, the fist remains.

fist.name: Empty Hand of Mushin
fist.found: Zen monastery in Japan, given by a master who sees Usagi's discipline
fist.description: Not a weapon — a state of mind. Mushin: 'no-mind.' The absence of fear, anger, ego. The fist strikes without thought. +10% evasion, combo chain bonuses doubled.

fist.name: Galley Slave's Chains
fist.found: The chains of a freed galley slave at Lepanto
fist.description: Iron chains worn for years at the oar. When the battle was won, the slaves were freed. Some kept their chains — as a reminder.

fist.name: Bare-Knuckle Brawler's Wraps
fist.description: Blood-soaked hand wraps from the streets of Paris. The Revolution was fought with fists as much as with ideas.

fist.name: Kolbe's Blessing Hands
fist.found: Kolbe blesses Usagi's hands before his death
fist.description: Not gauntlets or wraps. Just hands. Hands that Kolbe blessed with his dying breath. Every strike heals allies for 10% of damage dealt.
fist.lore: The physical attack power is nearly nothing. The spiritual power is overwhelming. This is the Franciscan paradox made flesh.

fist.name: Doubting Thomas's Touch
fist.found: Unlocked by achieving Faith 10 after starting with Faith 0 at some point (the doubter's journey)
fist.description: Thomas touched the wounds and believed. These hands know what doubt feels like — and what lies on the other side. Every hit reveals all enemy weaknesses.
fist.lore: 'My Lord and my God.' Thomas went further than any other apostle — to India, tradition says. The doubter became the most daring missionary.

spear.name: Sharpened Pole
spear.description: A stick with a point. The oldest weapon in human history. Older than prayer. Almost.

spear.name: Soldier's Yari
spear.description: An ashigaru's spear. Functional, impersonal, made for a hand that follows orders.

spear.name: Bamboo Spear
spear.found: Cut and hardened from bamboo forest
spear.description: Fire-hardened bamboo with a sharpened tip. Light, fast, breaks easily. But bamboo grows back.

spear.name: Roman Pilum
spear.description: Heavy javelin designed to bend on impact so the enemy can't throw it back. Practical Roman engineering. Can be thrown for a ranged attack.

spear.name: Praetorian Lance
spear.found: Taken from a defeated Praetorian Guard
spear.description: The elite emperor's guard carried these. Perfectly balanced, perfectly maintained. The Praetorian didn't need it anymore.

spear.name: Desert Nomad's Spear
spear.found: Traded from desert nomads in Egypt
spear.description: Lightweight acacia wood with a bronze head. Designed for hunting jackals and worse things in the deep desert.

spear.name: Crusader's Lance
spear.description: Twelve-foot cavalry lance. Devastating on the charge. Less useful in tight quarters. First-strike damage +30%.

spear.name: Fragment of the Holy Lance
spear.found: Hidden in a Crusader reliquary beneath Acre, accessible only with Faith ≥ 6
spear.description: A shard from the lance that pierced Christ's side. Longinus stabbed — and from the wound flowed blood and water. The shard still weeps.
spear.lore: The game hints at this weapon's existence through NPC dialogue. Finding it requires exploration, faith, and listening to old stories.

spear.name: Mongol Cavalry Lance
spear.description: Light, curved, designed for mounted combat. The Mongols conquered more of the world than anyone. Their lances helped.

spear.name: Winged Hussar's Kopia
spear.found: Given by the Hussar commander before the charge at Liegnitz
spear.description: The hollow lance of the Polish Winged Hussars. Designed to shatter on impact, driving the point through armor. Charge damage +50%.
spear.lore: The Hussars charged into an army ten times their size. They knew they would die. They charged anyway. The kopia is courage made wood and iron.

spear.name: Hara Castle Naginata
spear.description: Polearm from the rebel fortress. The Christians of Shimabara held Hara Castle for months. This naginata was on the wall until the end.

spear.name: Galley Boarding Pike
spear.description: Short pike for ship-to-ship combat. You jam it through a gap in the enemy's shield wall. Ugly work.

spear.name: Revolutionary's Pike
spear.description: A citizen's weapon. Crude iron point on a long pole. The Revolution was built on pikes and pamphlets.

spear.name: Spear of Destiny
spear.found: Manifests in the final battle if Spear mastery 10, all virtues ≥ 5
spear.description: The full Lance of Longinus. Not a fragment — the whole thing. It exists because every prayer in the game has been building toward this moment.
spear.lore: History says a hundred armies fought over this relic. Usagi doesn't fight over it. It simply appears. That is the difference between power and grace.

bow.name: Hunting Bow
bow.description: Short bow for hunting rabbits. The irony is not lost on Usagi.

bow.name: Bamboo Daikyū
bow.description: Tall asymmetric Japanese bow. The grip is below center. It takes practice to aim correctly.

bow.name: Ashigaru's Hankyū
bow.description: Military-issue short bow. Mass-produced but reliable. The daimyō's archers are terrifyingly accurate. You are less so.

bow.name: Roman Composite Bow
bow.description: Layered horn and sinew. The auxiliaries — non-citizens recruited from the empire's edges — carried these. Better archers than the legionaries.

bow.name: Egyptian Recurve
bow.description: Elegant recurve bow from the Nile region. Fast to draw, accurate at medium range. The pharaohs hunted lions with these.

bow.name: Crusader's Crossbow
bow.description: Heavy crossbow. Slow to reload, devastating on impact. 20% armor penetration. The Pope once tried to ban crossbows. It didn't work.

bow.name: Mongol Composite Bow
bow.description: The deadliest ranged weapon of the medieval world. The Mongols could fire accurately at full gallop. Can fire while moving (no standing-still requirement for aimed shots).

bow.name: Master's Yumi
bow.found: Given by a retired samurai archer who recognizes Usagi's discipline
bow.description: A master-crafted longbow. The wood is seasoned for three years. The string is woven from specific silk. Every arrow from this bow finds its way. +10% crit.
bow.lore: The old archer said: 'Archery is not about the arrow. It is about the space between breaths. Fire in that space.'

bow.name: Venetian Naval Bow
bow.description: Composite bow designed for naval combat. The salt air warps most bows. This one is lacquered against the sea. +15% damage at maximum range.

bow.name: Resistance Marksman's Bow
bow.description: A silent weapon for a silent war. First shot from stealth deals +50% damage.

bow.name: Bow of Light
bow.found: Appears during the Rosary vision at Lepanto if Hope ≥ 5
bow.description: A bow that fires arrows of pure light. No quiver needed — it draws from prayer. The string hums with the Ave Maria.
bow.lore: The arrow of light does not kill. It reveals. Enemies hit by it have all buffs, illusions, and disguises stripped away.

bow.name: Star of Bethlehem Bow
bow.found: Manifests when Hope is 10 and Bow mastery is 10
bow.description: A bow that fires stars. Each arrow arcs upward and descends like the Star that guided the Magi. The wise men followed a light. So does this arrow.
bow.lore: The Star of Bethlehem led seekers to truth. This bow does the same — every arrow finds the most vulnerable point, the deepest weakness, the hidden lie.

bow.name: Sagitta Dei
bow.found: Only if Bow sacred ultimate 'Sagitta Dei' skill is unlocked
bow.description: The Arrow of God. A bow that doesn't fire arrows — it fires truth. Each shot tracks across any distance, any dimension, any deception. Against the Accuser, it strips one immunity permanently.
bow.lore: This is the rarest weapon in the game. It requires commitment to a single path across the entire playthrough. Most players will never see it. Those who do will never forget it.
